item.title: Tariffs are Trump’s perpetual leverage machine
item.description: And the US might find them hard to give up

item.title: Donald Trump, Mukesh Ambani and a $300bn mystery
item.description: The US president has trumpeted a ‘historic’ refinery deal but Reliance Industries is only planning a modest investment

item.title: Investors sought to pull $20bn from private credit funds in first quarter
item.description: Big groups including Apollo, Ares and Blackstone were hit with redemption requests at start of 2026

item.title: Shipping stalls as Tehran dictates terms in Hormuz
item.description: Despite US-Iran ceasefire deal fewer tankers passed the crucial waterway than during fiercest days of fighting

item.title: Ackman in talks to launch fund to bet on investor complacency
item.description: Strategy would echo doomsday trades that earned Pershing Square a big windfall during the pandemic

item.title: UK becomes top destination for US jet fuel after Gulf flow squeeze
item.description: Iran war stokes embrace of American exports as Britain’s aviation sector shifts reliance on Middle East supplies

item.title: White-collar industries bet on a secret weapon against AI: trust
item.description: Executives across finance and cyber defence map where Anthropic’s Claude “plug-ins” will — and won’t — change work

item.title: US oil exports to hit record as Iran war triggers race for supplies
item.description: ‘Armada’ of Asian tankers heads for American ports as energy shortages spread

item.title: CLSA curtails flagship Tokyo forum because of its Chinese parent
item.description: Famed investor gathering becomes ‘access day’ with new event launched in South Korea

item.title: ‘A toxic environment for children’ — inside the beauty ‘cosmeticorexia’ battle
item.description: Campaigners say cosmetics companies and retailers such as Sephora are increasingly targeting buyers under 18

item.title: Wirecard trial shows the risks of Germany’s slow legal process
item.description: Financial crime cases can be vulnerable to long delays and procedural gridlock

item.title: Oh! What a weird war for investors
item.description: Buying oil might be the right move now

item.title: Nike’s comeback may not come all the way back
item.description: Rehashing old hits and leaving shop shelves empty has enabled upstarts to steal a march

item.title: BlackRock’s ambitious India shake-up falls short
item.description: Assets under management at joint venture with Mukesh Ambani have declined since last year’s launch

item.title: Ditch ‘alarming’ risk warnings to spur UK retail investment, fund managers told
item.description: Trials show more balanced messages boost share of wealth in equities

item.title: Sheinbaum announces fracking plan as Mexico seeks to curb US gas reliance
item.description: Global energy shock prompts rethink of environmental concerns by world’s largest buyer of US natural gas

item.title: FirstFT: Middle East ceasefire under pressure over Israeli attacks on Lebanon
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: CK Hutchison takes Maersk to arbitration, and Meta releases new AI model

item.title: Wells Fargo whistleblower award slashed by Wall Street watchdog
item.description: SEC’s $53mn payout over fake accounts scandal is less than half the amount originally planned

item.title: NHS data chief pushes for deeper rollout of Palantir technology despite outcry
item.description: Ming Tang says the platform, which collates patient information in one place, is delivering ‘outstanding results’ in England

item.title: SEC names Gibson Dunn partner David Woodcock to run enforcement unit
item.description: Appointment comes as Wall Street watchdog accelerates business-friendly deregulation drive

item.title: Go to court and lose out on £9bn car finance redress scheme, says FCA boss
item.description: Comments by Nikhil Rathi position watchdog for stand-off with claims management companies and law firms

item.title: SEC chair says US states should lead on policing corporate behaviour
item.description: Comments by Paul Atkins come as Trump administration is seeking to loosen regulatory constraints

item.title: Haircut for taxpayers after lenders take control of struggling broadband provider
item.description: National Wealth Fund gave Gigaclear £240mn guarantee three years ago and is now the biggest shareholder

item.title: Our quest not to solve bitcoin’s great mystery
item.description: Because what is this, 2014?

item.title: The UK needs to make choices on tech strategy
item.description: As with Brexit, public anger could swing behind the idea that we need to take back control

item.title: Why the ‘SaaSpocalypse’ doomsayers are wrong
item.description: Every major tech platform shift follows the same pattern: short-term pain for long-term gain

item.title: Meta releases first AI model since Zuckerberg’s spending spree
item.description: Muse Spark ‘purpose-built’ for social media apps as investors question huge AI investment

item.title: How this oil shock unwinds
item.description: At a rate of 1mn barrels a day of overproduction, it would take not far off two years to make up the buffers

item.title: Billionaire son of ‘world’s richest banker’ challenges $35mn legal fees
item.description: Alberto Safra was charged more than $162,000 on one day alone by US law firm WilmerHale

item.title: Saudi Arabia’s vital East-West oil pipeline attacked
item.description: Conduit has become an economic lifeline for the kingdom since the near closure of the Strait of Hormuz
